# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const token = request.cookies.get('auth-token');

  if (!token) {
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  // You can replace this with your token validation logic
  const isValidToken = validateAuthToken(token);

  if (!isValidToken) {
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  // Proceed with the response if the token is valid
  return NextResponse.next();
}

// Dummy auth token validation function
function validateAuthToken(token: string | undefined): boolean {
  if (!token) return false;
  // Implement your token validation logic here
  return token === 'valid-token';
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected-path/:path*'],
};
